wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SparkClaw project overview section focused on the managed OpenClaw hosting platform. Explain the project's purpose as a zero-devops solution for deploying OpenClaw AI assistants, its value proposition of eliminating infrastructure complexity, and relationship to the broader OpenClaw ecosystem. Document the implementation approach using Bun monorepo architecture, the technology stack including Elysia, SvelteKit, Stripe, Railway, and PostgreSQL. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners explaining what SparkClaw solves and technical details for experienced developers covering the full-stack architecture. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'managed hosting', 'instance provisioning', and 'subscription-based billing'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the end-to-end user journey from signup to deployed OpenClaw instance. Document the business model, target audience segmentation, and competitive advantages. Include diagrams where appropriate to illustrate the system architecture and user workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Executive Summary
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SparkClaw executive summary section focused on the managed OpenClaw hosting platform's core value proposition. Explain the fundamental problem SparkClaw solves - the complexity of deploying and managing OpenClaw AI assistants without requiring DevOps expertise. Document the solution approach of providing zero-devops managed hosting with automated instance provisioning on Railway. Include the business value proposition emphasizing 'launch your own OpenClaw in minutes' and predictable pricing models. Provide concrete metrics showing the dramatic reduction in setup time from days/weeks to under 5 minutes. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including 'managed hosting', 'instance provisioning', and 'subscription-based billing'. Include practical examples demonstrating the transformation from complex self-hosted deployments to simple managed service consumption.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system overview documentation for SparkClaw's architecture. Explain the high-level system design showing how the monorepo structure separates concerns across web, api, and shared packages. Document the overall system flow from user authentication through Stripe billing to Railway instance provisioning. Include system boundaries, major components, and their relationships. Explain the technology stack choices including Bun runtime, Elysia framework, SvelteKit frontend, and Drizzle ORM. Provide system context diagrams showing how SparkClaw fits into the broader OpenClaw ecosystem. Address the managed hosting approach that eliminates devops complexity for OpenClaw AI assistants.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SvelteKit routing system. Document the route structure including the main landing page (/), authentication routes (/auth), pricing page (/pricing), and dashboard (/dashboard). Explain the file-based routing convention and how SvelteKit automatically generates routes from the src/routes directory structure. Detail the route parameters, query string handling, and dynamic segments. Document navigation patterns using SvelteKit's link components and programmatic navigation with goto(). Include route guards and authentication checks for protected routes. Explain layout components and how they wrap different route groups. Document error handling with error pages and loading states. Provide examples of route transitions, prefetching strategies, and SEO considerations for each route type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity relationship documentation for SparkClaw's database schema. Detail the 1:1 relationships between users and subscriptions, and users and instances, explaining how each user can have exactly one subscription and one instance. Document the 1:N relationships showing how users can have multiple OTP codes and sessions, with proper referential integrity enforcement through foreign key constraints. Explain the business logic behind these relationships, such as why subscriptions and instances are unique per user, and how OTP codes and sessions support authentication workflows. Include entity relationship diagrams showing cardinality and participation constraints. Document the Drizzle ORM relations configuration including foreign key mappings and cascade behaviors. Address data consistency requirements and how these relationships prevent data anomalies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Types and Interfaces
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the type system in the shared package. Document all TypeScript interfaces and types including User, OtpCode, Session, Subscription, and Instance interfaces with their field definitions, data types, and validation rules. Explain the distinction between select types (for reading from database) and insert types (for writing to database) using Drizzle ORM's InferSelectModel and InferInsertModel utilities. Detail domain-specific types including Plan union types ('starter' | 'pro' | 'scale'), SubscriptionStatus enums, and InstanceStatus enums. Document API response interfaces MeResponse and InstanceResponse with their nested object structures and optional fields. Include usage examples showing how these types are imported and used across web and API packages. Provide guidelines for extending types and maintaining type safety throughout the monorepo.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Routing & Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Elysia API routing architecture and middleware implementation. Document the main application entry point, CORS configuration, health check endpoint, and route registration patterns. Explain the modular routing structure including authentication routes (/auth/*), API routes (/api/*), and webhook routes (/webhook/*). Detail middleware implementation including CORS settings, authentication guards, CSRF protection, and request validation. Document the route organization strategy, parameter handling, and response formatting standards. Include examples of route registration, middleware chaining, and error propagation. Address security middleware including rate limiting, input sanitization, and access control. Provide guidance on extending the routing system and adding new endpoints following established patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: OTP Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the OTP (One-Time Password) management system. Detail the 6-digit numeric OTP generation algorithm, SHA-256 hashing implementation for secure storage, and 5-minute expiration mechanism. Explain the OTP record creation process including database storage, hash verification, and cleanup procedures. Document the OTP request flow from generation through validation, including rate limiting enforcement and error handling. Cover the OTP schema validation, input sanitization, and security measures against replay attacks. Include examples of OTP generation, verification workflows, and integration patterns with the authentication system. Address common issues like expired codes, invalid formats, and database connectivity problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Railway API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for SparkClaw's Railway API integration. Detail the GraphQL API implementation including authentication via Bearer tokens, query construction for service creation, domain management, and environment discovery. Document the railwayRequest helper function with error handling for HTTP status codes and GraphQL errors. Explain the service creation workflow using the serviceCreate mutation with proper input validation and response parsing. Cover domain assignment through serviceDomainCreate mutation and domain retrieval via service query. Document environment ID discovery for production deployments and fallback mechanisms. Include examples of GraphQL queries and mutations, error scenarios and their handling, rate limiting considerations, and API response validation. Address security considerations for API token management and request signing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stripe Integration Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Stripe integration setup in SparkClaw. Document the Stripe client initialization with proper API version configuration and environment variable validation. Explain product and price configuration for Starter ($19/month), Pro ($49/month), and Scale ($99/month) plans including price ID management and plan mapping. Detail the checkout session creation process including customer email handling, success/cancel URL configuration, and metadata attachment for user identification. Document the webhook endpoint configuration and signature verification process. Include examples of Stripe dashboard setup, product catalog configuration, and webhook secret management. Address testing strategies using Stripe CLI and webhook testing tools. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common integration issues including API key validation, webhook endpoint accessibility, and session creation errors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stripe Payment Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Stripe payment integration in SparkClaw. Detail the Stripe SDK initialization, API version configuration, and client instantiation patterns. Document the checkout session creation process including plan selection, pricing configuration, success/cancel URLs, and metadata handling. Explain the webhook event processing system covering signature verification, event construction, and security considerations. Detail the subscription lifecycle management including checkout completion handling, subscription updates, and cancellation processing. Document the automated provisioning trigger mechanism that activates instance deployment upon successful payment. Include error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and fallback procedures. Provide examples of webhook testing, event debugging, and integration validation. Address Stripe dashboard configuration, API key management, and environment variable requirements. Document subscription status synchronization with local database records and real-time status updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for SparkClaw development environment setup. Cover prerequisites including Node.js/Bun runtime requirements, TypeScript knowledge, and familiarity with modern web development. Document step-by-step installation instructions for setting up the Bun workspace, installing dependencies, and configuring environment variables. Explain the monorepo structure with packages/web, packages/api, and packages/shared. Provide detailed environment variable configuration for development including DATABASE_URL, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, RAILWAY_API_TOKEN, RESEND_API_KEY, and SESSION_SECRET. Include local development workflow with commands for starting frontend, backend, and database services. Document the initial project setup process, running migrations, and verifying the development environment. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide examples of basic API testing and frontend verification. Keep the content accessible to beginners while ensuring experienced developers can quickly get productive.

wiki_catalogs.name: Problem-Solution Fit
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SparkClaw problem-solution fit section focused on the specific pain points and targeted solutions. Document the detailed problems addressed including DevOps complexity, infrastructure management overhead, SSL certificate setup, database configuration, monitoring requirements, and backup maintenance. Explain how SparkClaw eliminates these barriers through automated provisioning, managed infrastructure, and simplified operations. Include quantitative data showing the time and cost savings compared to self-hosted deployments. Document the target market segments including indie developers, creators, agencies, and small businesses who lack DevOps resources but need AI assistant capabilities. Provide concrete examples of typical deployment scenarios that SparkClaw solves, including server provisioning, Docker configuration, SSL setup, and ongoing maintenance tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed component interaction documentation showing how SparkClaw's components communicate and collaborate. Document the data flow patterns from user authentication through session management to Stripe webhook processing and Railway instance provisioning. Explain the request/response patterns between packages/web and packages/api, including authentication flows and subscription management. Detail the event-driven architecture showing how background jobs handle long-running operations like instance deployment. Include sequence diagrams illustrating typical user workflows such as new user registration, returning user login, and instance management. Address error propagation, retry mechanisms, and fallback strategies between components.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the SvelteKit UI component library. Document reusable components including authentication forms, OTP verification components, subscription cards, and instance status displays. Explain component props, events, slots, and customization options. Detail the component hierarchy and composition patterns used throughout the application. Document form components with validation, error handling, and accessibility features. Include button components, modal dialogs, and notification systems. Explain component styling with Tailwind CSS classes and custom CSS variables. Document component state management patterns and how components communicate with parent components. Provide usage examples with code snippets showing proper component integration. Address component testing strategies and maintainability guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Schemas
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed table schema documentation for all SparkClaw database tables. Document the users table with UUID primary key, email uniqueness constraint, and timestamp fields for created/updated times. Detail the otp_codes table including hash-based OTP storage, expiration tracking, usage flags, and indexing strategy. Explain the sessions table with secure token management, foreign key relationships to users, and session expiration handling. Document the subscriptions table covering Stripe integration fields, plan management, status tracking, and unique constraints. Detail the instances table with Railway deployment tracking, status management, error handling, and URL management. Include field definitions, data types, constraints, default values, and validation rules. Document the purpose and business significance of each field within the overall system architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation Schemas
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Zod validation schemas used throughout the shared package. Document input validation schemas for authentication requests, subscription management, and instance operations. Explain output validation schemas for API responses and database operations. Detail schema composition patterns, custom validation functions, and error handling strategies. Include examples of schema usage in API routes and service layers. Document the relationship between request/response schemas and database operation schemas. Provide guidelines for adding new validation schemas and maintaining consistency across the monorepo. Address schema evolution, backward compatibility, and migration strategies for validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the authentication service implementation. Document the OTP-based authentication flow including send-OTP endpoint (/auth/send-otp) and verify-OTP endpoint (/auth/verify-otp). Explain OTP generation algorithms, expiration handling, and security measures. Detail session management including cookie configuration, session storage, and logout functionality (/auth/logout). Document rate limiting implementation to prevent abuse and brute force attacks. Explain the email service integration for OTP delivery and transactional email handling. Include request/response schemas, error handling patterns, and security considerations. Provide examples of authentication workflows, session lifecycle management, and integration with the frontend. Address common authentication issues, debugging techniques, and security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the session management system. Explain the session token generation using crypto.randomUUID(), HTTP-only secure cookie configuration with SameSite lax policy, and 30-day expiration handling. Document the session creation process including user ID association, token storage, and database cleanup procedures. Detail session validation during API requests, token refresh mechanisms, and session termination on logout. Cover security considerations including CSRF protection, secure cookie attributes, and session hijacking prevention. Include examples of session creation, validation workflows, and logout procedures. Address session persistence, concurrent session handling, and cleanup strategies for expired sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Provisioning Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the complete OpenClaw instance provisioning workflow. Document the end-to-end process from Stripe webhook trigger to operational instance deployment. Explain the provisionInstance function orchestration including database record creation with pending status, service creation attempts, and domain assignment. Detail the retry mechanism with exponential backoff using Math.pow(2, attempt) * 1000 timing. Document the fallback polling strategy when immediate domain assignment fails, including INSTANCE_POLL_MAX_ATTEMPTS and INSTANCE_POLL_INTERVAL_MS configurations. Explain the state transitions from pending to ready or error states with proper error handling and logging. Cover the database updates for service IDs, URLs, and status tracking. Include workflow diagrams showing decision points, retry loops, and success/failure paths. Address timeout handling, error propagation, and administrative recovery procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Checkout Session Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the complete Stripe Checkout session flow in SparkClaw. Document the step-by-step process from plan selection through payment completion including user journey mapping and UI flow. Explain checkout session creation with proper metadata handling for user identification and plan selection. Detail success and cancellation URL configuration with parameter handling and state management. Document the redirect flow from Stripe Checkout back to SparkClaw dashboard with success confirmation and error handling. Include examples of checkout session customization options, recurring billing setup, and subscription terms configuration. Address browser compatibility, mobile optimization, and fallback handling for checkout failures. Provide debugging techniques for checkout session issues and user experience optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Railway Cloud Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Railway cloud hosting integration. Document the GraphQL API integration including query construction, mutation execution, and response handling. Detail the service deployment process covering project creation, environment variable configuration, domain assignment, and build triggers. Explain the background job processing system for asynchronous provisioning operations including retry logic, timeout handling, and error recovery. Document the status monitoring mechanism for tracking deployment progress and health checks. Include examples of service configuration, environment management, and resource allocation. Address Railway dashboard integration, API key management, and project organization. Document the instance lifecycle management from creation to termination including scaling operations and maintenance procedures. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues, network connectivity problems, and service availability concerns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for SparkClaw's full-stack system design. Describe the high-level architecture including the Bun monorepo structure, separation of concerns between web, api, and shared packages, and integration patterns with external services. Document component interactions showing data flow from user authentication through Stripe billing to Railway instance provisioning. Explain technical decisions including the choice of Elysia for backend, SvelteKit for frontend, Drizzle ORM for database abstraction, and the event-driven provisioning architecture. Include infrastructure requirements with Railway for hosting, Neon PostgreSQL for database, Stripe for payments, and Resend for email. Document deployment topology showing how packages/web deploys to Vercel/Cloudflare Pages while packages/api runs on Railway. Address cross-cutting concerns like security (HTTPS, CSRF protection, session management), monitoring with Sentry and PostHog, and error handling strategies. Provide system context diagrams and component interaction diagrams. Explain scalability considerations, availability targets, and the roadmap for architectural evolution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Target Users
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SparkClaw target users section focused on the primary and secondary user personas. Document the primary target users including indie developers, creators, and small SaaS companies who want OpenClaw AI assistants but lack DevOps expertise. Detail the secondary target users including agencies and consultants who need to deploy AI solutions for multiple clients. Explain the specific pain points for each persona including setup complexity, maintenance overhead, and operational burden. Document the user journey from awareness to deployment, including decision-making factors and success criteria. Include behavioral characteristics, technical capabilities, and business requirements for each user segment. Provide concrete examples of how SparkClaw addresses the unique needs of different user types, from individual experimentation to enterprise-scale deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create deployment architecture documentation covering SparkClaw's multi-environment deployment strategy. Document how packages/web deploys to Vercel/Cloudflare Pages for static frontend delivery while packages/api runs on Railway for serverless backend execution. Explain the environment configuration management using Zod validation and process.env variables. Detail the containerization approach with Docker support and the build pipeline using Bun workspaces. Include infrastructure requirements showing Railway for compute, Neon PostgreSQL for database, Stripe for payments, and Resend for email services. Address deployment automation, CI/CD pipelines, and rollback strategies. Document monitoring setup with Sentry for error tracking and PostHog for analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for state management patterns in the SvelteKit application. Document Svelte stores including writable, readable, and derived stores for managing authentication state, user data, and application-wide state. Explain reactive declarations and how they handle component state updates. Detail session management using browser storage and how authentication state persists across page reloads. Document global state patterns for subscription data, instance status, and user preferences. Explain store subscriptions and cleanup patterns to prevent memory leaks. Include examples of local component state vs. global store state decisions. Document state synchronization with server-side data and handling of async state updates. Address debugging state management issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Indexes and Constraints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database indexes and constraints in SparkClaw's PostgreSQL schema. Detail the indexing strategy including unique indexes on email, stripe customer ID, and stripe subscription ID for optimal query performance. Document functional indexes on expires_at for OTP cleanup operations and status fields for filtering. Explain foreign key constraints enforcing referential integrity between users, sessions, subscriptions, and instances. Detail unique constraints preventing duplicate subscriptions and instances per user. Document check constraints and validation rules ensuring data integrity. Include performance implications of each index and constraint, query optimization benefits, and maintenance considerations. Address index usage patterns in common queries like authentication, subscription lookup, and instance status filtering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for environment variable validation and configuration management in the shared package. Document the Zod-based environment validation schema including all required variables for development, staging, and production environments. Explain validation rules, default values, and type safety guarantees. Detail the runtime configuration loading process and error handling for missing or invalid environment variables. Include examples of accessing validated configuration in different packages (web, api). Document security considerations for sensitive environment variables and best practices for configuration management across different deployment targets. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common configuration issues and validation errors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Billing & Subscription Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the billing and subscription management system. Document Stripe integration including checkout session creation, subscription management, and payment processing. Explain webhook endpoint (/api/webhook/stripe) implementation for handling Stripe events including payment success, failure, subscription cancellations, and plan changes. Detail subscription lifecycle management, automated provisioning triggers, and payment failure handling. Document pricing models, plan configurations, and usage-based billing calculations. Include webhook security implementation with signature verification and idempotent event handling. Provide examples of checkout flows, subscription updates, and webhook processing. Address common billing issues, refund handling, and subscription management workflows. Document integration patterns with the provisioning system and user notification mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Delivery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the email delivery system integrated with Resend. Explain the OTP email template structure, HTML formatting, and delivery mechanisms. Document the email sending workflow including error handling, retry logic, and delivery monitoring. Detail the integration with Resend API, authentication setup, and configuration management. Cover email content customization, branding considerations, and fallback strategies for failed deliveries. Include examples of email template usage, delivery tracking, and troubleshooting common email delivery issues. Address email validation, spam filtering considerations, and compliance with email deliverability standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Status Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the instance status management system. Document the four status states: pending, ready, error, and suspended with their meanings and transition triggers. Explain the status update mechanisms during provisioning including initial pending state, successful ready state with URL assignment, and error state with detailed error messages. Detail the database schema for status tracking including createdAt, updatedAt timestamps and status field updates. Document the polling mechanism for status verification with INSTANCE_POLL_MAX_ATTEMPTS and INSTANCE_POLL_INTERVAL_MS constants. Explain the relationship between status changes and UI updates in the dashboard interface. Cover error state handling with errorMessage storage and recovery procedures. Include status transition diagrams, state validation rules, and administrative controls for forced state changes. Address monitoring and alerting for stuck pending states and recurring error conditions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Webhook Event Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Stripe webhook event processing in SparkClaw. Document webhook security implementation including signature verification, webhook secret management, and event authenticity validation. Explain the three main webhook events: checkout.session.completed for successful payments, customer.subscription.updated for plan changes, and customer.subscription.deleted for cancellations. Detail event payload parsing, idempotent event handling, and retry mechanism implementation for failed webhook deliveries. Document the webhook endpoint configuration, request validation, and response handling patterns. Include examples of webhook testing using Stripe CLI, event replay functionality, and debugging techniques for webhook processing errors. Address security considerations including webhook endpoint protection, rate limiting, and monitoring for suspicious activity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Service Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the email service integration using Resend. Document the email template system including OTP generation, template rendering, and delivery workflows. Detail the transactional email handling covering authentication emails, subscription notifications, and system alerts. Explain the deliverability optimization strategies including sender reputation management, bounce handling, and spam filtering. Document the email validation processes for recipient addresses, template variables, and content formatting. Include examples of email configuration, template customization, and delivery tracking. Address email service security including API key protection, rate limiting, and compliance requirements. Document error handling for failed deliveries, retry mechanisms, and notification systems. Provide troubleshooting guides for common email delivery issues, domain verification problems, and inbox placement optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application (Web)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SvelteKit frontend application. Describe the component architecture including the routing system with pages for landing (/), authentication (/auth), pricing (/pricing), and dashboard (/dashboard). Document state management patterns using Svelte stores and reactive declarations. Explain the styling strategy with Tailwind CSS utility classes and component-based design. Detail authentication UI components including email input forms, OTP verification interfaces, and session management displays. Document the dashboard interface design showing subscription status cards, instance management panels, and real-time status indicators. Include component composition patterns and integration with shared types and constants. Address responsive design principles and mobile-first development approach. Document the build process, deployment configuration, and adapter settings for Vercel/Cloudflare Pages. Provide examples of component usage, prop interfaces, and event handling patterns. Include guidelines for extending the UI with new pages and components while maintaining consistency with existing design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SparkClaw technology stack section focused on the complete technical architecture and component selection rationale. Document the Bun monorepo architecture using workspaces for efficient development and deployment. Explain the frontend stack using SvelteKit for modern web application development with SSR and static site generation capabilities. Detail the backend architecture using Elysia framework for high-performance API development with TypeScript support. Document the database layer using PostgreSQL via Neon for serverless, auto-scaling database operations with Drizzle ORM for type-safe queries. Include the cloud infrastructure stack covering Railway for instance hosting, Stripe for payment processing, Resend for transactional emails, and Sentry for error tracking. Explain the deployment strategy using Vercel for frontend hosting and Railway/Fly.io for backend services. Document the reasoning behind each technology choice, including performance, developer experience, and operational benefits.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security patterns documentation for SparkClaw's security architecture. Document the authentication security model including OTP generation with expiration, rate limiting implementation, and secure session management with HttpOnly cookies. Explain CORS configuration, CSRF protection, and input validation strategies. Detail the security measures for external service integrations including Stripe webhook signature verification and Railway API authentication. Address data encryption at rest and in transit, secret management with environment variables, and audit logging. Include security best practices for email delivery, payment processing, and instance provisioning. Document vulnerability mitigation strategies and incident response procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Theming
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the styling architecture using Tailwind CSS and custom theming. Explain the Tailwind configuration including custom colors, spacing scales, and component variants. Detail the utility-first approach and how it's applied consistently across components. Document dark mode implementation and theme switching mechanisms. Explain responsive design patterns and mobile-first development approach. Detail custom CSS additions and how they integrate with Tailwind utilities. Document component-specific styling patterns and how they maintain consistency. Include guidelines for adding new styles while preserving design system coherence. Explain CSS-in-JS alternatives and when to use them. Address performance considerations for styling including critical CSS and bundle optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive migration management documentation for SparkClaw's database schema evolution. Detail the Drizzle ORM configuration including database connection settings, schema location, and migration options. Document the schema-first development approach using TypeScript definitions that generate SQL migrations. Explain the migration workflow from schema changes to database updates, including generation commands and deployment strategies. Detail the migration file structure and naming conventions used by Drizzle. Document rollback procedures and version management. Include best practices for safe schema changes, testing migration scripts, and handling production deployments. Address migration conflicts, incremental updates, and backward compatibility considerations. Provide examples of common migration scenarios and their implementation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Constants and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for constants and configuration values defined in the shared package. Document plan definitions including starter, pro, and scale tiers with their features, pricing, and limitations. Explain status enumerations for subscriptions, instances, and other domain entities. Detail error codes, HTTP status mappings, and error message templates. Document feature flags, toggle switches, and experimental feature configurations. Include API endpoints, database table names, and other hardcoded values used across the monorepo. Provide guidelines for adding new constants, maintaining consistency, and avoiding magic numbers in the codebase. Address versioning strategies for configuration changes and backward compatibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Instance Provisioning Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the instance provisioning service. Document Railway API integration including GraphQL queries, deployment orchestration, and instance lifecycle management. Explain background job processing architecture for asynchronous provisioning tasks, retry logic, and error handling. Detail domain assignment, status monitoring, and real-time provisioning updates. Document the provisioning workflow from payment confirmation to instance availability, including environment variable configuration and resource allocation. Include examples of provisioning requests, status polling, and error recovery mechanisms. Address provisioning failures, rollback procedures, and manual intervention capabilities. Document the integration with the dashboard system for real-time status display and user access. Provide troubleshooting guides for common provisioning issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rate Limiting & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the rate limiting and security measures implemented in the authentication system. Explain the dual-rate limiting system with different caps for OTP sending (3 per 10 minutes) and verification (5 per 15 minutes). Detail the IP-based rate limiting implementation, client IP detection from proxy headers, and key generation strategies. Document CSRF protection middleware, input validation patterns, and security middleware integration. Cover brute force attack prevention, account lockout mechanisms, and suspicious activity detection. Include examples of rate limiter usage, security middleware configuration, and custom security implementations. Address security best practices, vulnerability mitigation, and monitoring suspicious authentication patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Handling and Recovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for error handling and recovery mechanisms in the instance provisioning system. Document the retry logic implementation with exponential backoff strategy and maximum retry limits defined by INSTANCE_PROVISION_MAX_RETRIES. Explain error categorization including transient errors, permanent failures, and timeout conditions. Detail the error propagation mechanism through the retry loop with proper error message preservation. Document the fallback polling strategy when direct domain assignment fails, including polling intervals and maximum attempts. Explain error state management with status updates to 'error' and detailed error message storage. Cover timeout handling for deployment completion with graceful degradation to polling mode. Include error recovery procedures for administrators, manual intervention steps, and cleanup processes. Document logging strategies for debugging provisioning failures, monitoring error rates, and implementing circuit breaker patterns. Address edge cases like partial deployments, orphaned resources, and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Subscription Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for subscription lifecycle management in SparkClaw. Document subscription status tracking including active, past_due, canceled, and unpaid states with automatic status updates from Stripe. Explain proration handling for plan changes, usage-based billing adjustments, and pro-rata calculations. Detail subscription modification workflows including plan upgrades/downgrades, quantity changes, and billing cycle adjustments. Document customer portal integration for self-service subscription management and payment method updates. Include subscription data synchronization between Stripe and local database including conflict resolution and reconciliation processes. Address subscription analytics including renewal rates, churn prediction, and revenue forecasting. Provide examples of subscription state transitions, automated billing workflows, and customer communication strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring and Analytics Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for monitoring and analytics integrations including Sentry error tracking and PostHog analytics. Document the Sentry integration covering error capture, performance monitoring, and alerting configurations. Detail the structured logging implementation including log levels, context injection, and correlation tracking across service boundaries. Explain the analytics data collection including user behavior tracking, conversion funnel analysis, and feature usage metrics. Document the monitoring dashboard setup, alert configuration, and incident response procedures. Include examples of custom metrics, performance indicators, and operational KPIs. Address data privacy compliance, anonymization strategies, and retention policies. Document the integration with external monitoring tools, log aggregation systems, and alerting platforms. Provide troubleshooting guides for monitoring configuration issues, false positive alerts, and performance degradation detection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend API (API)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Elysia backend service. Document the route organization including authentication endpoints (/auth/send-otp, /auth/verify-otp, /auth/logout), subscription management (/api/checkout, /api/me), instance management (/api/instance), and webhook handlers (/api/webhook/stripe). Explain middleware implementation including authentication guards, CSRF protection, and request validation. Detail the service layer architecture covering OTP generation and validation, Stripe integration for payment processing, Railway API integration for instance provisioning, and database service abstractions. Document error handling patterns, logging strategies, and security implementations including rate limiting, session management, and input validation. Include request/response schemas for all endpoints with example payloads and expected responses. Address webhook security including signature verification and idempotent handling. Document the background job processing architecture for asynchronous provisioning tasks. Provide examples of API usage, authentication flows, and integration patterns. Include performance considerations, monitoring approaches, and troubleshooting guides for common API issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SparkClaw business model section focused on revenue generation and value delivery mechanisms. Document the subscription-based pricing model with three-tier plans (Starter, Pro, Scale) offering predictable monthly pricing with optional usage-based add-ons. Explain the value-delivery mechanism where customers pay for managed hosting services rather than infrastructure costs. Detail the operational efficiency model where SparkClaw handles all DevOps complexity, allowing customers to focus on building AI applications. Document the competitive advantages including elimination of infrastructure management, reduced operational risk, and simplified scaling. Include financial projections showing revenue streams, cost structures, and profitability assumptions. Explain the roadmap for monetization expansion including usage-based billing, premium features, and enterprise licensing. Provide concrete examples of how the business model creates value for both customers and SparkClaw stakeholders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SvelteKit build process and deployment configuration. Document the Vite build pipeline including asset optimization, code splitting, and bundle analysis. Explain adapter configuration for different deployment targets (Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, static hosting). Detail environment variable handling and build-time configuration. Document the development workflow including hot module replacement and fast refresh capabilities. Explain production optimizations including minification, tree shaking, and asset compression. Include deployment strategies and CI/CD integration patterns. Document performance monitoring and bundle size analysis tools. Address common build issues and troubleshooting techniques. Explain caching strategies and CDN integration for optimal performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for data access patterns and database interaction strategies in SparkClaw. Document the Drizzle client configuration and connection management including environment-based settings and connection pooling. Detail common query patterns for authentication workflows, subscription management, and instance status tracking. Explain transaction handling for critical operations like user registration and payment processing. Document bulk operations, batch processing, and performance optimization techniques. Include examples of complex queries with joins, aggregations, and filtering. Address caching strategies, query optimization, and monitoring approaches. Document error handling patterns, retry logic, and graceful degradation strategies. Provide guidelines for maintaining data consistency across distributed operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Abstraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the database abstraction layer using Drizzle ORM. Document all table schemas including users, otpCodes, sessions, subscriptions, and instances with their field definitions, data types, constraints, and relationships. Explain the database initialization process, connection management, and transaction handling. Detail query patterns, relationship definitions, and join operations. Document migration strategies, schema evolution, and database versioning. Include examples of CRUD operations, complex queries, and database utility functions. Address performance considerations, indexing strategies, and query optimization techniques. Provide guidelines for extending database models, adding new tables, and maintaining database consistency across the monorepo.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Service Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the database service layer and ORM implementation. Document the Drizzle ORM configuration, database schema definitions, and entity relationships. Explain the service abstraction layer for database operations, query patterns, and transaction management. Detail type-safe database operations, migration management, and schema evolution strategies. Document connection pooling, query optimization, and performance considerations. Include examples of CRUD operations, complex queries, and data validation. Address database security including prepared statements, input sanitization, and access control. Document backup strategies, monitoring approaches, and troubleshooting database issues. Provide guidelines for extending the schema, adding new entities, and maintaining data integrity across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Handling and Recovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for error handling and recovery mechanisms in SparkClaw's billing system. Document payment failure scenarios including declined cards, insufficient funds, and expired payment methods with appropriate user messaging and retry strategies. Explain webhook processing errors including signature verification failures, duplicate event handling, and timeout scenarios with automatic retry mechanisms. Detail subscription state inconsistencies and reconciliation processes for out-of-sync billing data. Document error logging, monitoring, and alerting systems for billing anomalies and payment processing failures. Include recovery procedures for failed instance provisioning triggered by webhook events. Address customer communication strategies for billing issues and escalation procedures for unresolved payment problems. Provide debugging tools and techniques for identifying and resolving billing system issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shared Package (Shared)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the shared package containing common utilities and types used across the monorepo. Document the type system including User, Subscription, Instance, and OTPCode interfaces with their field definitions, validation rules, and relationships. Detail the Zod validation schemas for input/output validation, including request/response schemas for API endpoints and database operations. Explain environment variable validation using Zod schemas for runtime configuration checking. Document constants and configuration values including plan definitions, status enumerations, error codes, and feature flags. Include utility functions and helper modules for common operations like hashing, formatting, and data transformation. Address the database abstraction layer using Drizzle ORM including table definitions, relationships, and query patterns. Document the import conventions and usage patterns for consuming shared modules in web and api packages. Provide examples of extending types, adding new validation schemas, and creating reusable utilities. Include guidelines for maintaining consistency across shared components and avoiding circular dependencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Handling & Logging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for error handling and logging systems. Document the centralized error handling architecture, custom error classes, and standardized error responses. Explain logging implementation with structured logging, log levels, and monitoring integration. Detail error boundary implementation, exception catching, and graceful degradation strategies. Document Sentry integration for error tracking, performance monitoring, and alerting. Include examples of error scenarios, logging patterns, and debugging techniques. Address common error categories, resolution strategies, and escalation procedures. Document logging best practices, log aggregation, and troubleshooting methodologies. Provide guidance on adding new error types, customizing error responses, and integrating with monitoring systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for SparkClaw's PostgreSQL database design. Detail the entity relationship model showing the 1:1 relationships between users, subscriptions, and instances, along with 1:N relationships for OTP codes and sessions. Document table schemas including users (UUID primary key, email uniqueness, timestamps), otp_codes (hash-based storage, expiration tracking), sessions (secure token management, foreign key relationships), subscriptions (Stripe integration fields, plan management), and instances (Railway deployment tracking, status management). Explain data validation rules, business constraints, and referential integrity enforcement. Include database schema diagrams showing entity relationships and cardinality. Document migration management using Drizzle ORM including schema-first development, migration generation, and deployment strategies. Address data access patterns, indexing strategies for query optimization, and performance considerations. Specify data lifecycle policies including OTP expiration, session cleanup, and audit trail preservation. Include data security measures, access control patterns, and privacy considerations. Document the connection management through Drizzle client and environment-based configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for SparkClaw's email-based authentication system. Explain the OTP generation and validation process including 6-digit numeric code generation, SHA-256 hashing for secure storage, and 5-minute expiration timing. Document the rate limiting implementation preventing abuse with OTP request caps (3 per 10 minutes) and verification attempt limits (5 per 15 minutes). Detail session management including HTTP-only secure cookies, 30-day expiration, SameSite attribute configuration, and token-based authentication. Explain the email service integration using Resend for transactional OTP delivery, including email templates, delivery monitoring, and fallback strategies. Document the complete authentication flow from email submission through OTP verification to session establishment. Address security considerations including CSRF protection, input validation, secure cookie attributes, and protection against brute force attacks. Include error handling patterns for invalid emails, expired codes, rate limit violations, and session management failures. Provide examples of authentication API usage, integration patterns, and troubleshooting common authentication issues. Document the logout process and session cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Billing and Subscription Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for SparkClaw's Stripe-powered billing and subscription management system. Document the Stripe integration setup including product configuration (Starter, Pro, Scale plans), price management, and checkout session creation. Explain the complete subscription flow from plan selection through Stripe Checkout redirection to payment completion and post-payment redirect. Detail webhook event processing including checkout.session.completed for triggering instance provisioning, customer.subscription.updated for plan changes, and customer.subscription.deleted for cancellation handling. Document webhook security including signature verification, idempotent event handling, and retry mechanisms for failed deliveries. Address subscription lifecycle management including status tracking (active, canceled, past_due), proration handling, and customer portal integration. Include error handling for payment failures, webhook processing errors, and subscription state inconsistencies. Document the integration with the provisioning system for automatic instance creation upon successful payment. Provide examples of webhook testing, subscription management APIs, and troubleshooting billing issues. Include monitoring and reporting capabilities for revenue tracking and subscription analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Instance Provisioning
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for SparkClaw's OpenClaw instance provisioning system. Document the Railway API integration including GraphQL API usage for service creation, environment variable configuration, and project management. Explain the provisioning workflow triggered by Stripe webhook events, including background job processing, retry logic with exponential backoff, and status polling mechanisms. Detail the instance lifecycle from initial creation through deployment completion to operational status. Document the template-based deployment using forked OpenClaw Docker images with pinned versions and environment variable injection. Address error handling and recovery including transient error retries, timeout management, and failure state reporting. Explain the status tracking system with pending, ready, error, and suspended states and their corresponding UI representations. Document the integration with the dashboard for displaying provisioning progress and instance URLs. Include monitoring and alerting for provisioning failures, manual intervention procedures, and administrative controls. Provide examples of provisioning API usage, troubleshooting deployment issues, and scaling considerations. Address the roadmap for improving provisioning reliability and reducing deployment times.

wiki_catalogs.name: External Integrations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for SparkClaw's external service integrations. Document the Stripe integration including payment processing, subscription management, webhook security, and customer portal functionality. Detail the Railway API integration covering GraphQL operations, service deployment, environment configuration, and project management. Explain the Resend email service integration for OTP delivery, transactional email handling, and deliverability optimization. Document the Sentry error tracking integration including error reporting, performance monitoring, and alerting configurations. Address the PostHog analytics integration for user behavior tracking, conversion funnel analysis, and feature flag management. Include integration security considerations including API key management, request signing, and data privacy compliance. Document monitoring and logging strategies across all external services including structured logging, error correlation, and performance metrics. Provide examples of integration testing, failure scenarios, and recovery procedures. Address vendor lock-in mitigation strategies and alternative provider configurations. Include troubleshooting guides for common integration issues and service outage scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for SparkClaw project contributors. Document code style standards including TypeScript configuration, ESLint rules, and formatting conventions. Explain testing strategies including unit testing patterns, integration testing approaches, and end-to-end testing methodologies. Detail the development workflow including branch management, pull request procedures, and code review processes. Document environment configuration management including local development setup, staging environments, and production deployment procedures. Address deployment procedures for both frontend and backend services including CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and rollback strategies. Include monitoring and observability practices using Sentry, PostHog, and custom logging. Document performance optimization techniques, security best practices, and accessibility guidelines. Provide guidelines for extending the system including adding new features, integrating additional services, and maintaining backward compatibility. Include troubleshooting guides for common development issues, debugging techniques, and performance profiling. Address team collaboration practices including documentation standards, communication protocols, and knowledge sharing procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation for all SparkClaw endpoints. Document authentication endpoints including POST /auth/send-otp for OTP generation, POST /auth/verify-otp for session creation, and POST /auth/logout for session termination. Detail subscription management endpoints including POST /api/checkout for Stripe Checkout session creation and GET /api/me for user information retrieval. Document instance management endpoints including GET /api/instance for instance status and details. Explain webhook endpoints including POST /api/webhook/stripe for Stripe event processing with supported event types (checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted). For each endpoint, document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error codes. Include detailed parameter descriptions, validation rules, and example request/response pairs. Address security considerations including CSRF protection, rate limiting, and webhook signature verification. Document response formats, status codes, and error handling patterns. Provide client implementation examples in multiple programming languages and integration guidelines. Include performance considerations, rate limiting information, and best practices for API consumption.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation and frequently asked questions for SparkClaw. Document common issues and their solutions including authentication problems (invalid OTP, session expiration, rate limit violations), billing issues (payment failures, webhook processing errors, subscription status inconsistencies), and provisioning problems (deployment failures, timeout issues, environment configuration errors). Explain debugging techniques including log analysis, error tracking with Sentry, and monitoring dashboard usage. Address performance optimization including database query optimization, API response time improvements, and frontend performance enhancements. Document security best practices including proper environment variable management, secure cookie configuration, and input validation. Include step-by-step troubleshooting guides for complex scenarios like multi-service failures, data inconsistency resolution, and emergency recovery procedures. Provide FAQ sections addressing common user questions about pricing, feature limitations, migration procedures, and support resources. Document monitoring and alerting configurations for proactive issue detection. Include escalation procedures for critical issues and coordination with external service providers. Address preventive maintenance procedures and system health checks.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# SparkClaw: Managed OpenClaw Hosting Platform

## Project Introduction

SparkClaw is a managed hosting platform designed to eliminate the complexity of deploying and managing OpenClaw AI assistants. The project addresses a significant pain point for developers, creators, and small businesses who want to leverage OpenClaw's powerful AI capabilities without dealing with infrastructure management.

### Purpose Statement
SparkClaw provides a turnkey solution that automatically provisions OpenClaw instances on modern cloud platforms, eliminating the need for users to manage servers, Docker configurations, SSL certificates, databases, or monitoring systems.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Zero-DevOps Deployment**: Enable users to launch their own OpenClaw instance in under 5 minutes
- **Managed Infrastructure**: Handle all cloud infrastructure provisioning and maintenance
- **Predictable Pricing**: Offer transparent monthly subscription plans with optional usage add-ons
- **Multi-Channel Support**: Support for 25+ messaging channels including Telegram, Discord, Slack, and LINE

### Target Audience
- **Primary**: Indie developers, creators, and small SaaS companies wanting AI-powered automation
- **Secondary**: Agencies and consultants managing multiple client deployments
- **Future**: Non-technical business owners seeking intelligent chatbot solutions

## Technical Architecture

SparkClaw follows a modern monorepo architecture built on cutting-edge technologies, designed for scalability and maintainability.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[SvelteKit Web Frontend]
    B --> C[Elysia API Backend]
    C --> D[Stripe Billing System]
    C --> E[Railway Cloud Platform]
    C --> F[Neon PostgreSQL Database]
    E --> G[OpenClaw Instance]
    
    H[Resend Email Service] --> C
    I[Sentry Error Tracking] --> C
    J[PostHog Analytics] --> B
    
    K[Drizzle ORM] --> F
    L[Bun Runtime] --> C
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of four main layers:

**Frontend Layer**: SvelteKit-based web application providing user interfaces for authentication, dashboard, and pricing pages.

**Backend Layer**: Elysia-powered API server handling business logic, authentication, billing integration, and instance provisioning.

**Infrastructure Layer**: Cloud-native services including PostgreSQL database, email delivery, error tracking, and analytics.

**External Services**: Third-party integrations for payments, cloud hosting, and operational monitoring.

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

- **Monorepo Pattern**: Single codebase managing multiple packages through Bun workspaces
- **Microservice Architecture**: Separation of concerns across distinct packages (web, api, shared)
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Background job processing for asynchronous operations like instance provisioning
- **Repository Pattern**: Database access abstraction through Drizzle ORM
- **Factory Pattern**: Service creation and configuration management

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Web
    participant API
    participant Stripe
    participant Railway
    participant Database
    
    User->>Web: Sign up for service
    Web->>API: Authenticate user
    API->>Database: Store user data
    User->>Web: Choose subscription plan
    Web->>API: Create checkout session
    API->>Stripe: Create checkout session
    Stripe-->>User: Redirect to payment
    User->>Stripe: Complete payment
    Stripe->>API: Webhook notification
    API->>Railway: Provision OpenClaw instance
    Railway-->>API: Instance ready
    API->>Database: Update instance status
    API-->>Web: Dashboard with instance URL
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**API Entry Point**: `packages/api/src/index.ts`
- Initializes Elysia application with CORS configuration
- Sets up environment validation and logging
- Configures core middleware and route registration

**Web Application Entry Point**: `packages/web/svelte.config.js`
- Configures SvelteKit with TypeScript support
- Sets up Tailwind CSS and Vite build system
- Defines route handlers and page components

**Shared Package Entry Point**: `packages/shared/src/index.ts`
- Exports common types, schemas, and database utilities
- Provides centralized configuration and constants

### Core Modules

**Authentication System**: `packages/api/src/services/otp.ts`
- Implements email-based authentication using 6-digit OTP codes
- Handles rate limiting and security measures
- Manages session creation and validation

**Billing Integration**: `packages/api/src/services/stripe.ts`
- Creates Stripe Checkout sessions for subscription purchases
- Processes webhook events for payment confirmation
- Manages subscription lifecycle events

**Instance Provisioning**: `packages/api/src/services/railway.ts`
- Orchestrates OpenClaw instance deployment on Railway
- Implements retry logic and error handling
- Manages background job processing

**Database Abstraction**: `packages/shared/src/db/schema.ts`
- Defines PostgreSQL table structures using Drizzle ORM
- Establishes relationships between entities
- Provides type-safe database operations

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**: `packages/shared/src/env.ts`
- Validates required environment variables
- Defines type-safe configuration objects
- Supports development and production environments

**Build Configuration**: `tsconfig.json`
- TypeScript compiler options for consistent builds
- Module resolution and path mapping
- Source map generation for debugging

**Database Configuration**: `drizzle.config.ts`
- Drizzle Kit configuration for schema generation
- Migration management and database connectivity
- PostgreSQL-specific settings

### External Dependencies

**Runtime Environment**: Bun (JavaScript runtime)
- Provides fast execution and built-in package management
- Enables monorepo workspace support
- Offers excellent performance for server-side applications

**Frontend Framework**: SvelteKit (React-like component system)
- Server-side rendering with static site generation
- Built-in TypeScript support and hot module replacement
- Optimized for modern web standards

**Backend Framework**: Elysia (Lightweight API framework)
- Minimal overhead with excellent performance
- Native TypeScript support and plugin ecosystem
- Event-driven architecture suitable for long-running processes

**Database Layer**: PostgreSQL via Neon
- Serverless PostgreSQL with auto-scaling capabilities
- Connection pooling and performance optimization
- Support for advanced PostgreSQL features

**ORM Solution**: Drizzle ORM
- Type-safe SQL query builder
- Schema-first development approach
- Migration management and database versioning

### Integration Points

**Payment Processing**: Stripe Checkout and Webhooks
- Hosted checkout experience eliminates PCI compliance concerns
- Real-time subscription status synchronization
- Automated provisioning triggers on successful payments

**Cloud Infrastructure**: Railway Platform
- Programmatic deployment through GraphQL API
- Automatic scaling and health monitoring
- Integrated environment variable management

**Communication Services**: Resend (Transactional Email)
- OTP delivery with reliable deliverability
- Template-based email system
- Integration with email authentication flows

**Monitoring and Analytics**: Sentry and PostHog
- Comprehensive error tracking and performance monitoring
- User behavior analytics and conversion funnel tracking
- Structured logging for operational insights

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Web Frontend] --> B[API Backend]
    B --> C[Database Layer]
    B --> D[External Services]
    
    D --> E[Stripe Payments]
    D --> F[Railway Hosting]
    D --> G[Resend Email]
    D --> H[Sentry Monitoring]
    
    C --> I[Drizzle ORM]
    I --> J[PostgreSQL Database]
    
    B --> K[Session Management]
    B --> L[Rate Limiting]
    B --> M[CSRF Protection]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Zero-Configuration Deployment**: Users can instantly access their OpenClaw instance after payment confirmation, with automatic provisioning handled entirely by the system.

**Multi-Channel Integration**: Support for 25+ messaging platforms including Telegram, Discord, Slack, LINE, WhatsApp, and web chat interfaces.

**Subscription Management**: Flexible monthly billing with three-tier pricing structure supporting different usage levels and feature sets.

**Real-Time Status Monitoring**: Live dashboard showing instance provisioning progress, status updates, and operational metrics.

### Implementation Highlights

**Authentication Flow**: `packages/api/src/routes/auth.ts`
- Email-based authentication with 6-digit OTP verification
- Session management with secure cookies and CSRF protection
- Rate limiting to prevent abuse and ensure system stability

**Provisioning Pipeline**: `packages/api/src/services/railway.ts`
- Asynchronous instance creation with retry logic
- Background job processing for long-running operations
- Health monitoring and error reporting

**Dashboard Interface**: `packages/web/src/routes/dashboard/+page.svelte`
- Real-time subscription and instance status display
- One-click access to OpenClaw setup and console
- Responsive design optimized for various devices

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticating: Email OTP
    Authenticating --> Unauthenticated: Invalid OTP
    Authenticating --> Authenticated: Valid OTP
    
    Authenticated --> Subscribing: Choose Plan
    Subscribing --> PaymentProcessing: Stripe Checkout
    PaymentProcessing --> Provisioning: Payment Success
    PaymentProcessing --> Subscribing: Payment Failed
    
    Provisioning --> Ready: Instance Online
    Provisioning --> Error: Provisioning Failed
    Ready --> [*]: Logout
    Error --> Provisioning: Retry
    Error --> [*]: Contact Support
```

**Security Features**: Comprehensive security implementation including HTTPS enforcement, input validation, rate limiting, CSRF protection, and secure session management.

**Performance Optimization**: Built-in caching strategies, efficient database queries, and optimized API responses to ensure fast user experiences.

**Scalability Design**: Cloud-native architecture supporting horizontal scaling, load balancing, and automatic resource allocation based on demand.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [tsconfig.json](tsconfig.json)
- [drizzle.config.ts](drizzle.config.ts)
- [PRD.md](PRD.md)
- [packages/api/src/index.ts](packages/api/src/index.ts)
- [packages/api/src/routes/api.ts](packages/api/src/routes/api.ts)
- [packages/shared/src/db/schema.ts](packages/shared/src/db/schema.ts)
- [packages/shared/src/types.ts](packages/shared/src/types.ts)
- [packages/shared/src/schemas.ts](packages/shared/src/schemas.ts)
- [packages/api/package.json](packages/api/package.json)
- [packages/web/package.json](packages/web/package.json)
- [packages/shared/package.json](packages/shared/package.json)

</blog>
